title: Auto-trigger fan-out on event raise (outbox: raise -> immediate dispatch)
business_reason: FR-NOTIFY-FANOUT-0014 deferred auto-trigger — events were raised into notification_events but only fanned out when the drain endpoint was called (latency / manual). Dispatch immediately when an event is raised, so recipients are reached without waiting for a drain. Outbox pattern: persist the event first, then fan out best-effort; the drain endpoint remains the backstop.

design.helper: server/notifications/fanout.ts raiseNotificationEvent(supabase, values, actor): insert the event, then best-effort fanoutEvent (try/catch — the persisted event is the source of truth, the drain endpoint retries). A duplicate idempotency key is a no-op.
design.generators: gen_effects.py (chains) + gen_actions.py (CRM follow-up) now emit raiseNotificationEvent instead of a raw notification_events insert — so EVERY raise site auto-dispatches. Regenerated transitionEffects.ts + leadService.ts (both generated — edited the generators, not the output).

param_14.question: When is a raised notification event fanned out?
param_14.answer: Immediately at raise time. raiseNotificationEvent persists the event then dispatches it (best-effort); the drain endpoint is the backstop if dispatch fails or for events raised by other paths. Proven: recording a CRM follow-up creates the recipient batch with no drain call.
param_14.answer_source: outbox pattern; notification_events raise sites (chains.json CHAIN-002, school_crm record_interaction)
param_14.security_implications: dispatch is best-effort and never breaks the raising transaction (the transition/interaction already applied); the persisted event + the idempotent drain guarantee at-least-once delivery; no client involvement.
param_14.approval_implications: none (event batches are queued from approved templates as in FR-NOTIFY-FANOUT-0014).
param_14.downstream_dependencies: a background worker could replace/augment the inline best-effort dispatch for heavy fan-outs; lead_owner resolver still maps to no recipient (resolver-mapping follow-up).
param_14.assumptions: raise sites pass the actor; generators own the raise emission.
